Bash(/tmp/check_untested.py << 'EOF'
import os
import re

app_dir = r"E:\\Sach\\Sua\\AI_v1\\maritime-ai-service\\app"
test_dir = r"E:\\Sach\\Sua\\AI_v1\\maritime-ai-service\\tests\\unit"

# Get all app modules
app_modules = {}
for root, dirs, files in os.walk\(app_dir\):
    for file in files:
        if file.endswith\('.py'\) and file != '__init__.py':
            full_path = os.path.join\(root, file\)
            module_path = full_path.replace\(app_dir, ''\).replace\('\\\\', '/'\).lstrip\('/'\)
            # Get line count
            with open\(full_path, 'r', encoding='utf-8', errors='ignore'\) as f:
                lines = len\(f.readlines\(\)\)
            app_modules[module_path] = \(full_path, lines\)

# Get all test files and extract what they import/test
test_modules = set\(\)
for file in os.listdir\(test_dir\):
    if file.endswith\('.py'\) and file.startswith\('test_'\):
        with open\(os.path.join\(test_dir, file\), 'r', encoding='utf-8', errors='ignore'\) as f:
            content = f.read\(\)
            # Extract imports
            for match in re.finditer\(r'from app\\.\([^ ]+\) import|import app\\.\([^ ]+\)', content\):
                mod = match.group\(1\) or match.group\(2\)
                test_modules.add\(mod.split\('.'\)[0] + '/' + mod.split\('.'\)[1] if '.' in mod else mod\)
                test_modules.add\(mod.replace\('.', '/'\) + '.py'\)
                test_modules.add\(mod + '.py'\)

# Find untested modules with >50 lines
untested = []
for mod_path, \(full_path, lines\) in sorted\(app_modules.items\(\)\):
    if lines > 50:
        found = False
        for test_mod in test_modules:
            if mod_path.replace\('/', '_'\).replace\('.py', ''\) in test_mod or test_mod in mod_path:
                found = True
                break
            # Also check if file name appears in content of any test
            mod_name = os.path.basename\(mod_path\).replace\('.py', ''\)
            if mod_name in test_mod or mod_name.replace\('_', ''\) in test_mod.replace\('_', ''\):
                found = True
                break
        if not found:
            untested.append\(\(mod_path, lines, full_path\)\)

# Sort by line count descending
untested.sort\(key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True\)

print\("UNTESTED MODULES \(>50 lines\):\\n"\)
for mod_path, lines, full_path in untested[:20]:
    print\(f"{lines:4d} lines | {mod_path}"\)
EOF)

Bash(/tmp/check_modules.py << 'EOF'
import os
import re

# Get all Python files from app/
app_dir = r"E:\\Sach\\Sua\\AI_v1\\maritime-ai-service\\app"
test_dir = r"E:\\Sach\\Sua\\AI_v1\\maritime-ai-service\\tests\\unit"

app_modules = []
for root, dirs, files in os.walk\(app_dir\):
    # Skip __pycache__ and __init__ files
    dirs[:] = [d for d in dirs if d != '__pycache__']
    for f in files:
        if f.endswith\('.py'\) and f != '__init__.py':
            full_path = os.path.join\(root, f\)
            relative_path = os.path.relpath\(full_path, app_dir\)
            app_modules.append\(relative_path.replace\('\\\\', '/'\)\)

# Get all test files
test_files = []
for f in os.listdir\(test_dir\):
    if f.endswith\('.py'\) and f.startswith\('test_'\) and f != '__init__.py':
        test_files.append\(f\)

# Read test files and find what they import from app
tested_modules = set\(\)
for test_file in test_files:
    path = os.path.join\(test_dir, test_file\)
    with open\(path, 'r', encoding='utf-8', errors='ignore'\) as f:
        content = f.read\(\)
        # Find all imports from app
        imports = re.findall\(r'from app\\.\(\\S+\)', content\)
        for imp in imports:
            # Normalize to file path
            tested_modules.add\(imp.replace\('.', '/'\)\)

# Find untested modules \(excluding small files and __init__\)
untested = []
for mod in sorted\(app_modules\):
    if mod == '__init__.py' or '__pycache__' in mod:
        continue
    # Check if this module is tested
    is_tested = False
    for tested in tested_modules:
        if mod.startswith\(tested.replace\('/', '.'\).replace\('.py', ''\)\):
            is_tested = True
            break
    if not is_tested:
        full_path = os.path.join\(app_dir, mod\)
        if os.path.exists\(full_path\):
            with open\(full_path, 'r', encoding='utf-8', errors='ignore'\) as f:
                lines = len\([l for l in f.readlines\(\) if l.strip\(\) and not l.strip\(\).startswith\('#'\)]\)
                if lines > 100:  # Only modules with 100+ meaningful lines
                    untested.append\(\(mod, lines\)\)

# Sort by line count descending
untested.sort\(key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True\)

print\("TOP UNTESTED MODULES \(100+ lines\):"\)
for mod, lines in untested[:15]:
    print\(f"{lines:4d} lines  {mod}"\)
EOF)
